commands.repair_reason: Needs contributor revision, owner/security review, and missing real behavior proof; this is not safe for automated repair while the branch mixes a sanitizer fix with a sensitive export command.; Needs attention: The diff has concrete security-boundary concerns in shared guarded fetch and the new Telegram-visible context export command.; Review finding: [P1] Keep URL sanitizing out of the shared fetch guard — src/inf... (sha=39f863661ca583065d9fd2f602fd92327abb3c8d)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds sentinel-token sanitizers for bash exec, web-fetch URLs, and file-tool path inputs, plus a bundled portable-context Telegram export command.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main passes exec commands to shell execution and validates web_fetch URLs with new URL(params.url), so leaked leading sentinels can cause the reported syntax or URL failures. I did not establish a live Gemma reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor revision, owner/security review, and missing real behavior proof; this is not safe for automated repair while the branch mixes a sanitizer fix with a sensitive export command.
